question_title: What will be the value of "Overnight Unsecured AMERIBOR Benchmark Interest Rate" on 2026-03-26?
summary: AMERIBOR is an overnight unsecured interbank rate that closely tracks the Federal Funds Effective Rate (currently flat at 3.64%). The last 10 AMERIBOR observations (Feb 27 - Mar 12) show an extremely stable regime with mean 3.664 and std of only 0.0017. The Fed meets March 17-18 with a 99.6% probability of holding rates at 3.50-3.75%, meaning AMERIBOR should remain anchored near its current level of ~3.663% through March 26. The main sources of uncertainty are: (1) the tiny probability of a surprise Fed rate change, (2) quarter-end proximity effects (March 26 is 5 days before quarter-end), and (3) daily random variation with very low volatility (~0.003/day). Monte Carlo simulation with 200k paths confirms a very tight distribution centered near 3.664.

factors.description: Fed holding rates at 3.50-3.75% on March 18 (99.6% market probability). AMERIBOR closely tracks Fed Funds rate with ~2.3bp spread. No rate change means AMERIBOR stays near current level.
AMERIBOR in extremely stable regime: 10 observations with std of only 0.0017, mean 3.664. Daily changes are tiny (vol ~0.003). Last value 3.6628.
Geopolitical risk from Iran conflict elevating oil prices to $120/bbl. Middle East tension could add money market stress, but AMERIBOR is anchored by Fed policy, not commodity prices.
Quarter-end proximity (March 26 is 5 days before quarter-end). Overnight rates can see mild upward pressure near quarter-end due to bank balance sheet constraints.
Small probability of surprise Fed cut (~0.4%) would drop AMERIBOR by ~25bp to ~3.41. Very unlikely but creates a left tail in the distribution.

reasoning:
**Forecast: AMERIBOR on March 26, 2026**

**Median estimate: ~3.665%** (essentially unchanged from current 3.663%)

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P10 | 3.649 |
| P20 | 3.655 |
| P40 | 3.662 |
| P60 | 3.668 |
| P80 | 3.675 |
| P90 | 3.680 |

**Key reasoning:**
- AMERIBOR tracks the Fed Funds rate very closely (current spread ~2.3bp)
- The Fed meets March 17-18 with **99.6% probability of holding** at 3.50-3.75%
- The rate has been in an ultra-stable regime (std = 0.0017) for the last 10 observations
- The distribution is very tight because the underlying rate is policy-anchored and policy isn't changing
- Minor upside risk from quarter-end proximity; minor downside risk from the negligible chance of a surprise Fed cut
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series AMERIBOR once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=AMERIBOR`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-03-26 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.
